title: Improve Search Performance

story.so_that: I can search for active tactics efficiently
story.i_want: receive first page search results within 3 seconds

journey.before: User needs to search through active tactics but experiences slow search response times that impact productivity
journey.during: User submits search query with filters and waits for results to be returned from the database
journey.after: User receives search results within 3 seconds and can view, filter, or refine their search with new tactics immediately visible

capability_breakdown.core_action: Optimize search performance to return first page results within 3 seconds while maintaining existing filter functionality
state_changes: Search results are updated when new tactics are created
Database query performance is improved through indexing or query optimization

acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am an Internal Requester When I use any existing search filters Then the filters continue to work as before And the search results are returned within 3 seconds
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Apply search filters without performance degradation

acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am an Internal Requester and I previously completed a search and a NEW Tactic was just created When I search again Then the new tactic should be included in the search results AND the search results should be returned within 3 seconds
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: See newly created tactics in search results

acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a user submits a search query When the search completes Then results are returned within 3 seconds
Given existing API consumers When they call search endpoints Then the API contracts remain unchanged

edge_cases.gherkin: Given I am an Internal Requester When I submit a search with empty or whitespace-only query Then results are returned within 3 seconds
Given I am an Internal Requester When I submit a search with extremely long query (1000+ characters) Then results are returned within 3 seconds
Given I am an Internal Requester When I submit a search with special characters (regex, SQL, etc.) Then results are returned safely within 3 seconds
Given I am an Internal Requester When I apply maximum number of filters at once Then results are returned within 3 seconds
Given the database contains very large number of tactics (10k+) When I search Then first page of results is returned within 3 seconds
Given multiple users are searching simultaneously (10+ concurrent) When each submits a search query Then each receives results within 3 seconds
Given a new tactic was just created When I search for it immediately Then it appears in results within 1 second

questions.question: What is the current baseline search performance?
What optimization approach is preferred: database indexing, query rewriting, caching, or combination?
Are there constraints on database schema changes (adding indexes)?
What is the expected size of the tactic dataset?
Should search results be cached, and if so, what is the acceptable cache staleness?
Are there specific search filter combinations that are performance bottlenecks?

domain_knowledge: What constitutes 'active tactics' and how they are filtered
Current search filter types and their implementation
Business rules for tactic visibility and access
stakeholder_questions: What is the current average search response time?
What are the most common search filters used?
Is there a preference for optimization approach?
What is the expected dataset size?
Are there any constraints on database schema changes?

recommendation: Proceed with implementation. This is a moderately complex performance optimization story with clear acceptance criteria. The main uncertainties are around the optimization approach and current baseline performance, which should be clarified with stakeholders before implementation begins.
